records.rawExplanation:
*2021 (peak-IP-cycle year — Q1 ◎/◎):* stock ¥1,700 (Jul 9) – ¥2,420 (Feb 10); year-end close ¥1,966.7 (no clean prior-year-close YoY anchor). **FY3/2021 actual: revenue ¥332,532M, operating profit ¥47,202M, net profit ¥26,942M** per Tempest cache. Calendar 2021 was a content-strong year for SE — broad cross-platform franchise pipeline, no major flagship miss yet, valuation rerated alongside Japanese game-publisher cohort. Sign summary: revenue rose; operating profit rose; net profit rose; shares ranged ¥1,700–¥2,420 with close near mid-range; business strong, stock strong. 業績 ◎ / 株価 ◎.

records.rawExplanation:
*2022 (peak earnings + Embracer divestiture — Q1 ◎/◎ with strategic action):* stock ¥1,655 (Apr 22) – ¥2,383 (Nov 8); year-end close ¥2,043.3 (+3.9% YoY). **FY3/2022 actual: revenue ¥365,317M (+9.9% YoY), operating profit ¥59,303M (+25.6% YoY), net profit ¥51,030M (+89.4% YoY)** per Tempest cache — peak OP/NP levels in the 5-year window. **NAMED STRATEGIC EVENT: May 1, 2022 — Square Enix sold Crystal Dynamics, Eidos-Montréal, Square Enix Montreal and IPs Deus Ex / Legacy of Kain / Thief to Embracer Group for $300M** per independent gaming-press coverage (Kotaku, Engadget). Sign summary: revenue rose +9.9%; operating profit rose +25.6%; net profit rose +89.4% (peak NP); Embracer divestiture closed May 2022; shares ranged ¥1,655–¥2,383, close +3.9% YoY. 業績 ◎ (peak OP + named divestiture executed) / 株価 ◎ (close +3.9%, range respected).

<<CANONICAL FY2022>>

records.name: Square Enix

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation:
*2023 (FFXVI release + Forspoken flop — Q4 ✕/✕, the flagship-miss year):* stock ¥1,551 (Dec 22) – ¥2,522 (Jun 21); year-end close ¥1,687.7 (−17.4% YoY). Stock peaked ¥2,522 on June 21, ONE DAY BEFORE Final Fantasy XVI launched (June 22, 2023), then declined steadily through Dec 22 trough ¥1,551 — a classic peak-launch-then-disappointment trajectory. **FY3/2023 actual (reported May 2023 — pre-FFXVI window): revenue ¥343,261M (−6.0% YoY), operating profit ¥44,302M (−25.3% YoY), net profit ¥49,341M (−3.3% YoY)** per Tempest cache. Forspoken (launched January 2023, Q4 FY3/2023) was an early-2023 flop that began the decline. **NAMED PRODUCT EVENT: Final Fantasy XVI launched June 22, 2023; SE initially announced 3M units shipped first week (positive framing); August 1, 2023 (Q1 FY3/2024 earnings) — Bloomberg reported SE CEO Takashi Kiryu acknowledged FFXVI sales "did not meet the high end of expectations", blamed slow PS5 adoption.** **Independent third-party signal real-time in 2023**: share price collapsed from the June 21 peak ¥2,522 — by mid-September 2023, SE had erased roughly $2B of market value (≈ −30% off the June peak); on the Q1 FY3/2024 earnings call (early August 2023) an analyst stated to investors that the Final Fantasy franchise's profitability was weakening and would take time to improve; gaming-press independent coverage tracked reception continuously through 2H 2023. These were the contemporaneous independent signals — not the 3.5M cumulative-sales figure that became public in March 2025 (Yasuda / SE-disclosed retrospective, NOT a real-time 2023 number). Sign summary: revenue fell −6.0% YoY (FY3/2023); operating profit fell −25.3% YoY; net profit fell −3.3% YoY; Forspoken Q4 flop + FFXVI underperformance contemporaneously signaled by share-price collapse + analyst-call statement on franchise profitability + ~$2B market-value erosion by mid-September; shares fell sharply to ¥1,551 multi-year low (Dec 22) on FFXVI reception; close −17.4% YoY. 業績 ✕ (FY3/2023 rev/OP/NP all down) / 株価 ✕ (close −17.4% on FFXVI flagship miss).

<<CANONICAL FY2023>>
業績_sign: ✕
株価_sign: ✕

op_dir: down
net_dir: down

records.rawExplanation:
*2024 (content-abandonment writedown + restructuring announcement — Q3 ✕/◎, market pricing in turnaround):* stock ¥1,460 (Jun 14) – ¥2,269 (Feb 16); year-end close ¥2,049 (+21.4% YoY). **FY3/2024 actual (reported May 2024): revenue ¥356,326M (+3.8% YoY), operating profit ¥32,601M (−26.4% YoY), net profit ¥14,891M (−69.8% YoY)** per Tempest cache — major net profit collapse on content-abandonment writedown. **NAMED IMPAIRMENT EVENT: March 2024 — Square Enix announced ¥22.1B (~$140M) content-abandonment writedown after deciding to be more selective with game development; multiple unannounced titles cancelled** per independent gaming-press coverage (Wikipedia, Engadget, Push Square). Sign summary: revenue rose +3.8% YoY; operating profit fell −26.4% YoY; net profit fell −69.8% YoY (collapse on content-abandonment ¥22.1B writedown); shares rallied to ¥2,269 high (Feb 16) early in year, fell to ¥1,460 mid-year trough (Jun 14), recovered to close ¥2,049 (+21.4% YoY) as market priced in the restructuring announcement. 業績 ✕ (NP collapse on content-abandonment writedown) / 株価 ◎ (close +21.4% YoY — market interpreted restructuring as positive turnaround signal despite NP collapse).

<<CANONICAL FY2024>>

records.rawExplanation:
*2025 (restructuring-margin-recovery delivers — Q1 ◎/◎ year):* stock ¥2,023 (Jan 24) – ¥3,861.7 (Aug 18); year-end close ¥2,859.5 (+39.6% YoY). **FY3/2025 actual (reported May 2025): revenue ¥324,495M (−8.9% YoY), operating profit ¥40,599M (+24.5% YoY), net profit ¥24,393M (+63.8% YoY)** per Tempest cache — restructuring-driven margin recovery (revenue down on the deliberate selectivity, but OP and NP up sharply on margin expansion). **NAMED STRATEGIC EVENT: November 2025 — additional layoffs in UK/US announced as SE accelerates refocus on Japanese-development pipeline** per Pure Xbox / Engadget independent coverage. Sign summary: revenue fell −8.9% YoY (deliberate selectivity from FY24 content-abandonment); operating profit rose +24.5% YoY; net profit rose +63.8% YoY (margin recovery); UK/US workforce reduction announced Nov 2025; shares rallied to ¥3,861.7 high (Aug 18) on the restructuring-margin-recovery signal; close +39.6% YoY. **Split-signal year: revenue down (deliberate restructuring) / profit up (margin recovery) — analyst-grade ambiguity, but the dominant theme is margin-recovery-on-deliberate-selectivity, same shape as Square Enix's prior restructuring cycles.** 業績 mixed (rev down on deliberate selectivity / OP+NP up on margin recovery — writer judgment is ◎ on the profit-acceleration evidence) / 株価 ◎ (close +39.6%, breaking out of the post-FFXVI range).

<<CANONICAL FY2025>>
業績_sign: ◎
株価_sign: ◎
revenue_dir: down
op_dir: up
net_dir: up
stock_high_date: 2025-08-18
stock_high_price: 3861

Cautious side: FY3/2025 revenue down −8.9% YoY is a deliberate-selectivity outcome from the March 2024 content-abandonment writedown, NOT a demand-side weakness — the strategic distinction the senior named for SE-vs-Collabos 2025 (SE = genuine restructuring; Collabos = reactive cost-cutting on shrinking business). The Q1 ◎/◎ designation for 2025 rests on op_dir + net_dir UP — both verified at Tempest source. Revenue down is captured honestly in revenue_dir. **Retrospective sales-magnitude data point (not load-bearing for any quadrant call): in March 2025 — almost two years after launch — SE disclosed FFXVI cumulative sales of ~3.5M; relayed by Hideki Yasuda / Toyo Securities. This 2025 retrospective number does NOT support the real-time 2023 lag claim and is documented here only as the source of the 3.5M figure if it appears in downstream commentary.** Two items NOT independently re-verified during this fetch and flagged for source-check at census time: (1) the ¥22.1B 2024 content-abandonment writedown magnitude, and (2) the +21.4% 2024 close that drives the Q3 ✕/◎ call.

Sources:
- Tempest local cache: data/tempest/9684/{company.json, financials.json}

- https://www.thegamer.com/final-fantasy-16-square-enix-financial-report-analyst-confirms-not-just-3-5-million-copies-since-launch/ (Hideki Yasuda / Toyo Securities — independent analyst commentary on FFXVI sales, including the retracted 3.5M figure)
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Square_Enix (May 2022 Embracer divestiture details + March 2024 ¥22.1B content-abandonment writedown)
- https://www.engadget.com/gaming/square-enix-is-laying-off-more-developers-in-the-uk-and-us-as-it-refocuses-on-japan-201907305.html (Nov 2025 UK/US layoffs coverage)
- https://www.purexbox.com/news/2025/11/square-enix-is-cutting-down-its-western-workforce-focusing-on-japanese-development (Nov 2025 Western workforce reduction, independent)

The Q1 ◎/◎ designation for 2025 rests on op_dir + net_dir UP — both verified at Tempest source. Revenue down is captured honestly in revenue_dir. **Retrospective sales-magnitude data point (not load-bearing for any quadrant call): in March 2025 — almost two years after launch — SE disclosed FFXVI cumulative sales of ~3.5M; relayed by Hideki Yasuda / Toyo Securities. This 2025 retrospective number does NOT support the real-time 2023 lag claim and is documented here only as the source of the 3.5M
